name: AKS Primality Test
description: AKS: First unconditional deterministic polynomial-time primality test. Key: n is prime iff (X+a)^n ≡ X^n+a (mod n, X^r−1) for small a. Steps: (1) n perfect power? (2) find small r with ord_r(n) > log² n; (3) check small a; (4) n ≤ r ⇒ prime. Time Õ(log^6 n). Landmark 2002 result.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A["Input: n"]
    B["n = a^b? → composite"]
    C["Find r: ord_r n > log² n"]
    D["a ≤ r: gcd a,n > 1? → composite"]
    E["For a=1 to ⌊√φ r log n⌋:\n(X+a)^n ≡ X^n+a mod n,X^r−1?"]
    F["Composite"]
    G["Prime"]
    A --> B
    B --> C
    C --> D
    D --> E
    E -->|No| F
    E -->|Yes| G
    classDef red fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    classDef green fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    classDef lavender fill:#e6e6fa,color:#333
    class A red
    class B,C,D,E,G green
    class E lavender
    class F red
